content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
January 12,
1993
Deborah Washington
City Ethics Commission
201 North Los Angeles Street
L.A. Mail
- Suite
2
Los Angeles,
CA
90012
RE:
Combined Campaign Report
Our File No.
1—93—25
Dear Ms.
Washington:
This is to confirm advice provided to you on January ii,
1993, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).l/
Section 84205 states:
The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit
candidates and committees to file campaign statements
combining statements and reports required to be filed by
this title.
Committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose
the qualification of a ballot measure must file a campaign
statement 21 days after the deadline for filing petitions.
(Section 84200.5(f).)
Effective January 1,
1991,
committees
primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification of a
ballot measure are required to file semi—annual campaign
statements during the qualification period in addition to the 21-
day post—qualification statement.
(Section 84200.)
You sent me a copy of a letter addressed to Ms.
Rebecca
Avila,
Deputy Director for Policy and Operations for the City
Ethics Commission from Reed
& Davidson dated December 16,
1992,
requesting permission to consolidate the filing of the Citizens
for L.A.
Term Limits’
21-day post qualification and semi-annual
campaign statements.
The Reed
& Davidson letter incorrectly
1/ Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95812
•
(916) 322-5660


Deborah Washington
Our File No.
1—93—21
Page
2
stated the deadline for filing the petitions was December 26.,
1992.
During our telephone conversation of January 5,
1993..,
you
indicated the deadline for filing the petitions. was December 28.,
1992.
The committee is required to file a semi—annual campaign
statement covering the period January 1,
1992 through.. December 31,
1992,
due January 31,
1993.
In addition,
based on the .December.
28th date, the committee is required to file a 21-day post-
qualification statement which would cover the period January 1
1993 through January 11,
1993,
due January 18,. 1993..
Because
January 18th is an official state. holiday,
the: due date is
extended to January 19,
1993.
Because the 21-day.post-
qualification statement and the semi—annual statement cover almost
identical periods, it would be appropriate to allow committees
primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification of. the
ballot measure to combine the semi—annual. statement. with. the
21—
day post-qualification statement.
Therefore,
we advised you that the combined statement should
cover the period January 1,
1992 through January11,
1993,
and
should be filed no later than January 19,
199.3.
If you have any questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at 916/322—5662.
Sincerely,
Ma
Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: Whether committees formed to support or oppose a ballot measure qualification can file a combined semi-annual campaign statement and 21-day post-qualification statement when the reporting periods substantially overlap.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that a combined statement covering January 1, 1992 through January 11, 1993 is appropriate and should be filed no later than January 19, 1993, given the substantially identical periods of the two required statements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formally labeled QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; information extracted from letter body. File reference number inconsistency noted (1-93-25 in header, 1-93-21 on page 2). No formal question or conclusion sections present, but advice is clearly articulated in letter format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether committees formed to support or oppose a ballot measure qualification can file a combined semi-annual campaign statement and 21-day post-qualification statement when the reporting periods substantially overlap.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that a combined statement covering January 1, 1992 through January 11, 1993 is appropriate and should be filed no later than January 19, 1993, given the substantially identical periods of the two required statements.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission January 12, 1993 Deborah Washington City Ethics Commission 201 North Los Angeles Street L.A. Mail - Suite 2 Los Angeles, CA 90012 RE: Combined Campaign Report Our File No. 1—93—25 Dear Ms. Washington: This is to confirm advice provided to you on January ii, 1993, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).l/ Section 84205 states: The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this title. Committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose the qualification of a ballot measure must file a campaign statement 21 days after the deadline for filing petitions. (Section 84200.5(f).) Effective January 1, 1991, committees primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification of a ballot measure are required to file semi—annual campaign statements during the qualification period in addition to the 21- day post—qualification statement. (Section 84200.) You sent me a copy of a letter addressed to Ms. Rebecca Avila, Deputy Director for Policy and Operations for the City Ethics Commission from Reed & Davidson dated December 16, 1992, requesting permission to consolidate the filing of the Citizens for L.A. Term Limits’ 21-day post qualification and semi-annual campaign statements. The Reed & Davidson letter incorrectly 1/ Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, . All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660 Deborah Washington Our File No. 1—93—21 Page 2 stated the deadline for filing the petitions was December 26., 1992. During our telephone conversation of January 5, 1993.., you indicated the deadline for filing the petitions. was December 28., 1992. The committee is required to file a semi—annual campaign statement covering the period January 1, 1992 through.. December 31, 1992, due January 31, 1993. In addition, based on the .December. 28th date, the committee is required to file a 21-day post- qualification statement which would cover the period January 1 1993 through January 11, 1993, due January 18,. 1993.. Because January 18th is an official state. holiday, the: due date is extended to January 19, 1993. Because the 21-day.post- qualification statement and the semi—annual statement cover almost identical periods, it would be appropriate to allow committees primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification of. the ballot measure to combine the semi—annual. statement. with. the 21— day post-qualification statement. Therefore, we advised you that the combined statement should cover the period January 1, 1992 through January11, 1993, and should be filed no later than January 19, 199.3. If you have any questions regarding this matter, please contact me at 916/322—5662. Sincerely, Ma Kvasager Political Reform Consultant
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming permission for Citizens for L.A. Term Limits to file a combined semi-annual and 21-day post-qualification campaign statement due to overlapping reporting periods, with specific guidance on the coverage dates and filing deadline of January 19, 1993.
